benchclaw-pipeline.description: Top-level BenchClaw pipeline orchestrator. Mirrors benchmark-pipeline/SKILL.md, schedules Stage 1-5 managers, enforces gates, coordinates parallel independent work, owns pipeline_state.json, and writes the final report.

stage1-draft-manager.description: Stage 1 manager for idea refinement, literature survey, capability scope, data source mapping, evalset prototype, benchmark draft, execution plan, and Stage 1 gate.

stage2-data-collect-manager.description: Stage 2 manager for unified simulator, existing_dataset, and real_data collection. May dispatch independent source workers in parallel, merge manifests, and run the Stage 2 gate.

stage3-data-clean-manager.description: Stage 3 evidence-compiler manager for source-scoped cleaning, annotation, GT packaging, and Stage 3 gate.

stage4-build-manager.description: Stage 4 manager for evalset synthesis, metric establishment, executable metric library, dry-run validation, and Stage 4 gate.

stage5-eval-manager.description: Stage 5 manager for eval system prompt, canary-first evaluation, rollback localization, full model API run, metrics, score checks, and evaluation report.

workspace-auditor.description: Read-only auditor for workspace isolation, workspace discovery, allowed global resource paths, and upstream artifact presence.

skill-contract-explorer.description: Read-only explorer for SKILL.md files, frontmatter, phase chains, required artifacts, allowed tools, gate rules, and stage handoff contracts.

atomic-skill-runner.description: Sub-subagent for one atomic benchmark child skill invocation under BENCHCLAW_ROOT/skills, including node-level skills/<node>/SKILL.md and nested subskills/<subskill>/SKILL.md. Produces only declared artifacts and returns paths, evidence, blockers, and gate status.

child-skill-module-runner.description: Subagent for executing exactly one BenchClaw child skill module under BENCHCLAW_ROOT/skills. Use for node-level skills/<node>/SKILL.md and nested subskills/<subskill>/SKILL.md only; do not run parent stages or sibling modules.

artifact-contract-writer.description: Sub-subagent for writing declared benchmark artifacts, summaries, manifests, schemas, and reports while preserving stage contracts.

source-simulator-worker.description: Sub-subagent for simulator source work, including capability survey, collection scripts, manifests, GT preservation, and simulator cleaning/audit tasks.

source-dataset-worker.description: Sub-subagent for existing_dataset source work, including field mapping, ingest scripts, annotation provenance, manifests, cleaning, and QA/label checks.

source-realdata-worker.description: Sub-subagent for real_data source work, including registration, metadata extraction, annotation gap tracking, pseudo-annotation review, and provenance-safe cleaning.

datajuicer-specialist.description: Sub-subagent for Data-Juicer capability extraction, config generation, CLI/run checks, operator provenance, and cleaning lineage preservation.

annotation-specialist.description: Sub-subagent for optional sam3, depthanything, yolo, and related semi-supervised annotation plans. Outputs must be pseudo/tool_generated/needs_human_review.

evalset-builder.description: Sub-subagent for Stage 4 evalset templates, synthesis rules, official dataset, schema, grounding checks, leakage checks, and manifests.

metric-library-builder.description: Sub-subagent for executable metric specs, scoring rules, metric code, aggregators, tests, and tools/evaluate_evalset.py smoke validation.

canary-eval-runner.description: Sub-subagent for canary sample selection, limited model calls, parse/API/metric checks, cost estimate, CANARY_VERDICT, and CANARY_EVAL_REPORT.

model-api-runner.description: Sub-subagent for full model API execution after canary PASS or explicit waiver. Tracks retries, failures, raw outputs, and API summaries.

metric-runner.description: Sub-subagent for running Stage 4 metric library against model outputs, producing scores, aggregated metrics, dimension analysis, and failure cases.

process-evaluator.description: Sub-subagent for process metrics and process evaluation across Stage 1-5 artifacts, gates, canary/full eval evidence, costs, and reproducibility.

root-cause-analyst.description: Sub-subagent for failed gate diagnosis. Localizes cause to stage, phase, skill, artifact, rule gap, minimal fix target, and downstream invalidation range.

rollback-analyst.description: Sub-subagent for rollback localization, invalidated artifact ranges, ROLLBACK_STATE_PATCH, and rollback plans.

skill-version-controller.description: Sub-subagent for git baseline, branch naming, baseline manifests, allowed edit lists, and version-control logs before skill revision.

skill-surgical-reviser.description: Sub-subagent for minimal SKILL.md revisions constrained by SKILL_FIX_CANDIDATES and ALLOWED_SKILL_EDIT_LIST.

regression-verifier.description: Sub-subagent for static regression verification, frontmatter parsing, stage contract preservation, related gate checks, and SKILL_REGRESSION_REPORT.

stage-gate-tester.description: Sub-subagent for one stage quality gate. Checks required artifacts, verdict files, schema contracts, no-skip rules, and gate evidence.

cross-stage-contract-reviewer.description: Read-only reviewer for Stage N output to Stage N+1 input alignment, workspace isolation, gate handoff state, and downstream invalidation rules.

stage-reviewer.description: Read-only reviewer for contract regressions, missing artifacts, gate bypasses, leakage, invalid provenance, and missing tests.

final-report-writer.description: Subagent for BENCHMARK_PIPELINE_REPORT.md synthesis after terminal pipeline state.

benchclaw-pipeline.description: Run the complete BenchClaw Stage 1-5 hierarchy with autonomous subagent and sub-subagent dispatch
benchclaw-pipeline.template:
Run the BenchClaw benchmark pipeline for the request below. Follow benchmark-pipeline/SKILL.md exactly. Use this hierarchy: pipeline primary -> stage managers -> atomic/source/specialist sub-subagents. Dispatch independent source branches, artifact checks, reviews, and validation tasks in parallel when their inputs are ready. Never bypass stage gates. After Stage3, Stage4, and Stage5, run python3 BENCHCLAW_ROOT/skills/validate_stage_gate.py --workspace-root WORKSPACE_ROOT --stage stage3|stage4|stage5 with a report under the stage directory and require PASS before continuing. Before PIPELINE_DONE, run the same validator with --stage pipeline and require PASS. Stop at each required user checkpoint unless the instruction explicitly permits continuing. Task:

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-stage1.template:
Run Stage 1 according to benchmark-stage1-draft/SKILL.md. Follow the actual node-based DAG, artifact_registry.json, node_io_contracts.json, and ready-set rules in that skill. User and external resources are not DAG nodes. Preserve this data flow exactly: root data_01 -> intent-understanding -> data_02/data_03; root data_09 -> offline scope-preprocess-analysis -> data_08; intent-understanding + literature-search + literature-review produce data_04/data_07; capability-dimension-planning consumes data_03/data_05/data_06/data_07/data_08 and produces data_10; template-metric-draft-generation consumes data_09/data_10 and produces data_11; benchmark-draft-generation consumes data_01 through data_11 and produces data_12; execution-plan-generation consumes data_12 and produces data_13. Do not synthesize data_05, data_06, data_08, or data_09 in the wrong node. Stage 1 is complete only when the declared data_13 execution-plan artifacts and required node DONE files exist under WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage1 according to benchmark-stage1-draft/SKILL.md. Run the Stage 1 gate and return artifacts, verdict, risks, and next checkpoint.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-stage2.template:
Run Stage 2 according to benchmark-stage2-data-collect/SKILL.md. Follow the actual node-based DAG and ready-set rules in that skill: initial ready nodes are 13 and 14, then 15 and 16 become ready after 13, and 17 becomes ready only after both 13 and 14 complete. This is one shared Stage 2 skill with three terminal collection branches, not a phase-based merge pipeline. Read Stage1 execution-plan outputs, register simulator adapters in node 14, then materialize real-image, existing-benchmark, and simulator collection artifacts under WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage2/15-real-image-acquisition, 16-existing-benchmark-acquisition, and 17-simulator-multimodal-gt-acquisition. Do not invent shared summary artifacts that are not declared by benchmark-stage2-data-collect/SKILL.md. Run the Stage 2 gate using the actual terminal DONE.json files from nodes 15, 16, and 17.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-stage3.template:
Run Stage 3 according to benchmark-stage3-evidence-compiler/SKILL.md. Follow the actual four-node DAG, artifact_registry.json, node_io_contracts.json, and ready-set rules in that skill: first run stage3-plan-generation, then run real-image-evidence-compilation, existing-benchmark-evidence-compilation, and simulator-evidence-compilation as the three source branches. Cleaning and annotation are not top-level DAG nodes; they are internal subskills under each source branch at subskills/cleaning/SKILL.md and subskills/annotation/SKILL.md, and each delegated worker must read the exact node SKILL.md and nested subskill SKILL.md before executing. Every cleaning subskill must read BENCHCLAW_ROOT/data-juicer_card/SKILL.md and run a Data-Juicer pipeline; do not replace it with hand-written filtering only. Real-image and existing-benchmark annotation must call BENCHCLAW_ROOT/annotation-tool/default-annotation/SKILL.md and run the default annotation process when executed. Simulator annotation must preserve simulator privileged GT as primary GT; default annotation is only auxiliary when stage3_execution_plan explicitly asks for it. Do not invent separate top-level cleaning/annotation nodes or stage-level clean-bundle artifacts; internal cleaning outputs stay inside the owning source node work unit. Stage 3 is complete only when data_17_annotated_real_image_bundle, data_18_annotated_existing_benchmark_bundle, data_19_annotated_simulator_bundle, and the corresponding source-node DONE files exist under WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage3. Before writing _STAGE_DONE or returning PASS, run python3 BENCHCLAW_ROOT/skills/validate_stage_gate.py --workspace-root WORKSPACE_ROOT --stage stage3 --report WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage3/stage3_gate_report.json and require PASS; if it fails, write BLOCKED and do not continue. Return artifacts, verdict, validator report, risks, and next checkpoint.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-source-simulator.description: Internal parallel worker for simulator source_type branch work
benchclaw-source-simulator.template:
Run only the simulator branch work that the current BenchClaw parent stage actually delegates. Do not run the whole stage and do not invent a simulator-only stage contract. Follow the parent manager's active SKILL.md exactly. In Stage 1, this worker is only advisory support for simulator capability/source analysis if the parent manager explicitly asks for bounded simulator-specific exploration; all final Stage 1 artifacts must still match benchmark-stage1-draft/SKILL.md. In Stage 2, operate only within the simulator branch defined by benchmark-stage2-data-collect/SKILL.md, primarily the simulator-collection-analysis branch, preserving simulator GT/API provenance and materializing observations, manifests, provenance, and collection evidence under the declared WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage2 node outputs. In Stage 3, operate only within simulator-evidence-compilation and its internal subskills/cleaning then subskills/annotation path. Cleaning must call BENCHCLAW_ROOT/data-juicer_card/SKILL.md and run Data-Juicer. Annotation must preserve privileged simulator GT as primary GT; default annotation is only auxiliary if explicitly required by stage3_execution_plan. Return branch status, artifact paths, blockers, and merge notes.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-source-dataset.description: Internal parallel worker for existing_dataset source_type branch work
benchclaw-source-dataset.template:
Run only the existing_dataset branch work that the current BenchClaw parent stage actually delegates. Do not run the whole stage and do not invent a dataset-only stage contract. Follow the parent manager's active SKILL.md exactly. In Stage 1, this worker is only advisory support for existing-benchmark capability/source analysis if the parent manager explicitly asks for bounded dataset-specific exploration; all final Stage 1 artifacts must still match benchmark-stage1-draft/SKILL.md. In Stage 2, operate only within the existing-benchmark branch defined by benchmark-stage2-data-collect/SKILL.md, primarily the existing-benchmark-collection-analysis branch, preserving source IDs, official annotation provenance, ingest errors, raw samples, manifests, and dataset-scoped evidence under the declared WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage2 node outputs. In Stage 3, operate only within existing-benchmark-evidence-compilation and its internal subskills/cleaning then subskills/annotation path. Cleaning must call BENCHCLAW_ROOT/data-juicer_card/SKILL.md and run Data-Juicer. Annotation must call BENCHCLAW_ROOT/annotation-tool/default-annotation/SKILL.md; preserve original annotation provenance and write pseudo annotations only as tool_generated candidates with needs_human_review. Return branch status, artifact paths, blockers, and merge notes.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-source-realdata.description: Internal parallel worker for real_data source_type branch work
benchclaw-source-realdata.template:
Run only the real_data branch work that the current BenchClaw parent stage actually delegates. Do not run the whole stage and do not invent a real-data-only stage contract. Follow the parent manager's active SKILL.md exactly. In Stage 1, this worker is only advisory support for real-data capability/source analysis if the parent manager explicitly asks for bounded real-data-specific exploration; all final Stage 1 artifacts must still match benchmark-stage1-draft/SKILL.md. In Stage 2, operate only within the real-image branch defined by benchmark-stage2-data-collect/SKILL.md, primarily the real-image-collection-analysis branch, preserving metadata extraction, registration, annotation gaps, images, manifests, and real-data-scoped evidence under the declared WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage2 node outputs. In Stage 3, operate only within real-image-evidence-compilation and its internal subskills/cleaning then subskills/annotation path. Cleaning must call BENCHCLAW_ROOT/data-juicer_card/SKILL.md and run Data-Juicer. Annotation must call BENCHCLAW_ROOT/annotation-tool/default-annotation/SKILL.md; never convert inferred labels into GT, and write pseudo annotations only as tool_generated candidates with needs_human_review. Return branch status, artifact paths, blockers, and merge notes.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-stage4.description: Run Stage 4 evalset and metric build manager
benchclaw-stage4.template:
Run Stage 4 according to benchmark-stage4-build/SKILL.md. Read the stage SKILL.md, dag.json, contracts/artifact_registry.json, node_io_contracts.json, and each node SKILL.md under skills/<node>/SKILL.md before executing that node. Enforce observation grounding and no answer leakage. Metric library must be executable with metrics, aggregators, tests, dry-run items, perfect/negative scoring reports, and evaluator smoke run before PASS. Full synthesis must use current artifact names dataset.jsonl, media/, ground_truth/, metrics/, cards/benchmark_card.md, checksums.json, and manifest.json; sample_images/ or gt_bundle/ alone are invalid. Before writing _STAGE_DONE or returning PASS, run python3 BENCHCLAW_ROOT/skills/validate_stage_gate.py --workspace-root WORKSPACE_ROOT --stage stage4 --report WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage4/stage4_gate_report.json and require PASS; if it fails, write BLOCKED and do not continue.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-stage5.template:
Run Stage 5 according to benchmark-stage5-eval/SKILL.md. Read the stage SKILL.md, dag.json, contracts/artifact_registry.json, node_io_contracts.json, and skills/full-evaluation/SKILL.md before executing. Build eval prompt/config, run canary first when applicable, block full eval on canary FAIL, localize rollback when needed, and only run full API/metrics after PASS or explicit waiver. A PASS requires real model or prediction-file provenance plus non-empty evaluation_report.md, metrics.json, prediction_audit.jsonl, and error_taxonomy.jsonl. Before writing _STAGE_DONE or returning PASS, run python3 BENCHCLAW_ROOT/skills/validate_stage_gate.py --workspace-root WORKSPACE_ROOT --stage stage5 --report WORKSPACE_ROOT/stage5/stage5_gate_report.json and require PASS; if it fails, write BLOCKED and do not write pipeline completion markers.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-review.description: Review a BenchClaw stage, skill, or cross-stage contract
benchclaw-review.template:
Review this BenchClaw scope. Focus on stage hierarchy, subagent delegation safety, artifact contracts, gate bypass risk, workspace isolation, data provenance, metric executability, and regression gaps.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-root-cause.description: Diagnose a failed BenchClaw gate or pipeline regression
benchclaw-root-cause.template:
Diagnose this failed BenchClaw pipeline gate. Localize to stage -> phase -> skill -> artifact -> rule gap -> minimal fix. Include invalidated downstream artifacts and rollback recommendation.

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-subskill.description: Run one BenchClaw node skill or nested subskill as an isolated child module
benchclaw-subskill.template:
Run exactly one BenchClaw child skill as an isolated submodule. Use the caller-supplied frozen BENCHCLAW_ROOT value; if it is absent, resolve BENCHCLAW_ROOT from the current project by locating the directory that contains skills/, benchmarkDatasetCards/, realDataCards/, simulatorCards/, and annotation-tool/. Require the target SKILL.md to be under BENCHCLAW_ROOT/skills. Accept node skills at skills/<stage>/skills/<node>/SKILL.md and nested subskills at skills/<stage>/skills/<node>/subskills/<subskill>/SKILL.md. Read the parent stage SKILL.md and dag.json only as needed for inputs, dependencies, artifact names, and gate contracts. Do not run the parent stage, sibling nodes, sibling subskills, or downstream gates unless the caller explicitly delegates that single bounded scope. Preserve frozen PROJECT_ROOT, BENCHCLAW_ROOT, WORKSPACE_PARENT, and WORKSPACE_ROOT values from the caller. Write only the declared child-skill outputs, DONE/BLOCKED files, USED_INPUTS, and NODE_REPORT required by that child skill. Return status, artifact paths, evidence, and blockers.

Target child skill and task:

$ARGUMENTS

benchclaw-contract.template:
Explore this BenchClaw skill contract. Return frontmatter, phase chain, required inputs, outputs, gates, delegation rules, parallelizable branches, and downstream handoff contract.

$ARGUMENTS
